name: Jean-Luc Ponty
abstract: Jean-Luc Ponty (French pronunciation: [ʒɑ̃lyk pɔ̃ti]; born 29 September 1942) is a French jazz and jazz fusion violinist and composer. He is considered a pioneer of jazz-rock, particularly for his use of the electric violin starting in the 1970s.

concerts.label: Tribute: Stephane Grappelli

about_quotes.speaker: Bela Fleck
about_quotes.evidence: Like I’ve got this trio I’m supposed to do. It’s Stanley Clarke and Jean Luc Ponty... we’ve never played together
about_quotes.doc_title: Bela Fleck interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, December 7, 2004

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Malone
about_quotes.evidence: he calls to say I had the job playing with George Duke and Jean Luc Ponty on the tour in ’73.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom Malone interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 3, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Ralph Humphrey
about_quotes.evidence: Jean Luc Ponty was in the band, George Duke, keyboard player, was in the band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ralph Humphrey interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 11, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Gary Burton
about_quotes.evidence: It was me and Chick, John McLaughlin, Albert Mangelsdorff on trombone (a German musician), and Jean-Luc Ponty, the violinist. So we did this.
about_quotes.doc_title: Gary Burton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: George Benson
about_quotes.evidence: When we went to the French Riviera for the first time... And from France, it was Jean-Luc Ponty... Jean-Luc was on a t.v. show with us.
about_quotes.doc_title: George Benson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Ernie Watts
about_quotes.evidence: He has performed with Buddy Rich, Jean Luc Ponty, the Rolling Stones and the Tonight Show Orchestra.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ernie Watts interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 11, 2003
